dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 5821 IH: Rural Hospital Fairness Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. LaMalfa introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Ways and Means

form.official-title: To amend title XVIII of the Social Security Act to provide for the treatment of certain hospitals as critical access hospitals under the Medicare program.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Rural Hospital Fairness Act.

section.header: Providing for the treatment of certain hospitals as critical access hospitals under the Medicare program
section.text: Section 1820(h) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395i–4(h)) is amended by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

paragraph.header: Continuation of CAH status for certain facilities

subparagraph.text: A facility described in subparagraph (B) that does not meet the location requirement under subsection (c)(2)(B)(i)(I) (and was not certified by the State as being a necessary provider of health care services to residents in the area under subsection (c)(2)(B)(i)(II)) shall be deemed to have been certified by the Secretary under subsection (e) as a critical access hospital so long as such facility is otherwise eligible to be designated by the State as a critical access hospital under subsection (c) and meets any other criteria required by the Secretary under subsection (e)(3).
For purposes of subparagraph (A), a facility described in this subparagraph is a facility that—

clause.text: was designated as a critical access hospital before January 1, 2002;
as of December 31, 2024, was certified by the Secretary under subsection (e) as a critical access hospital; and

as of the date on which the facility was officially notified by the Secretary that it was no longer so certified, was located in a county (or equivalent unit of local government) in which no other hospital, critical access hospital, or rural emergency hospital was located.
